title: Problem Discovery Before Solution Commitment · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for problem discovery before solution commitment and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for problem evidence dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.
Observe or reconstruct the current workflow with roles, triggers, inputs, handoffs, delays, exceptions, and consequences.
Compare direct observation, interview, artifact, analytics, support, market, and assumption evidence for relevance and bias.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need
steps.instruction:
Order the Request Vs Need evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Request Vs Need assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need, keep Request Vs Need connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A neighborhood service requests a mobile app while observed handoffs suggest the delay comes from policy, language access, and incomplete intake information. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Request Vs Need has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Request Vs Need precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A neighborhood service requests a mobile app while observed handoffs suggest the delay comes from policy, language access, and incomplete intake information. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Separate the requested feature from the user need, stakeholder decision, current behavior, and desired outcome.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Request Vs Need, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A stakeholder solution request is already a validated problem statement.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the quiz Request Vs Need case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the quiz Request Vs Need case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the quiz Request Vs Need case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the quiz Request Vs Need case

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Discovery Noncommitment
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Discovery Noncommitment: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A neighborhood service requests a mobile app while observed handoffs suggest the delay comes from policy, language access, and incomplete intake information. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Record open questions and postpone feature commitment until the problem and constraints meet an explicit evidence threshold.

Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Discovery Noncommitment.
steps.why: Retrieving Discovery Noncommitment through the concrete problem evidence dossier case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Discovery Noncommitment, keep Discovery Noncommitment connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record open questions and postpone feature commitment until the problem and constraints meet an explicit evidence threshold.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Request Vs Need to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Request Vs Need.
